David Zynda.review_text: David is new to teaching FIN360, so the semester started out very hard. But, David was very adaptable and ended up removing short answer questions and providing many extra credit opportunities. As long as you understand the core concepts it is not bad, definitely go to office hours at the beginning if you are confused because material builds off!

This was his first time teaching this course so I will give him so grace. I like the set up of the class as it is 5 exams and 10 homeworks so it doesn't require that much time besides studying for exams. There is also 30 extra credit points. His lectures are slightly unorganized but most of the exams are similar to practice problems

great guy but seems to still be getting the hang of handling a large capacity undergraduate course. I think the class will continue to get better as he makes it his own. Lectures felt put together in a very odd and confusing way, seemingly overcomplicating concepts. If you get maths and fundamental finance theory the class is a walk in the park.

Feel grossly unprepared for completing homework assignments and tests. The lectures are impossible to follow and quite frankly inadequate at providing information necessary to complete assignments. Rarely have I felt the need to write a review of a professor, but no one knows what is going on and it shouldn't be this difficult.

This professor is not qualified to be teaching finance. Most of the lectures were spent on personal stories about his "partner" rather than course material. If you don't have a fast computer or a Mac, you're at a big disadvantage for the Ex Prep assignments; the professor won't compromise on this.

Aazam Virani.review_text: Aazam's class will most likely be your first Finance class at Eller. He does a great job of teaching the fundamental concepts which will carry on throughout future Finance classes. This has definitely been one of my favorite classes at Eller. Another nice aspect of his class is that he offers a good amount of extra credit so an A is attainable.

Virani was my favorite teacher this semester. Show up to class and take notes so that you don't freak out the night before the test. He is passionate about finance, which has inspired me to continue in finance. With his background and knowledge, he could be the CFO anywhere by now. I felt honored to have him as a teacher.

Good professor, very knowledgeable and patient. Tests are VERY similar to the practice tests and there are a ton of practice problems from review sessions that get posted to D2L. Final is cumulative but easier problems (to me) than other tests and all multiple choice. Attendance is important. Do the extra credit. Plan for HW sets to take some time.

Virani is probably the best professor I have ever had so far. He is extremely well spoken, and is willing to go through things in depth for those who actually ask. Very respectable and inspirational figure. The key to this class is to frequently go over everything. The more time you let the info sink in, the more it becomes second nature.
Not too bas for a finance class, i think its fair enough. professor is really helpful and especially office hours
Handsome teacher and knows what he's doing!!! take advantage of the extra credit it will help a lot

great professor, knowledgeable and notes are easy to learn from. Tests are difficult so take extra time to study but he is very helpful in class and during office hours. Does great job introducing finance topics and his lectures are entertaining (well as entertaining as a finance lecture could be). Def recommend.

Aazam is the GOAT, he is highly intelligent and knows what he is talking about. Tests are hard but if you study and pay attention you get can get a good grade. Aazam truly cares about his students and makes lectures entertaining. This class is really interesting as it introduces you to the basics of finance and Aazam does a great job at doing that.

Aazam was always engaging with the class even if we didn't answer back. He offers an insane amount of extra credit. He has 4 exams with the final being cumulative, which was not my favorite. The problem sets take a long time so budget yourself accordingly. Attendance was a large part of the overall grade which was a bit of a bummer.

I never went to class bc his lectures are awful, but still got an A in the class. The exams are identical to the study guides just with different wording/numbers, so those will be your biggest help. Go to class on exam review days bc he will tell you what problems you need to know how to do for the exam. Do ALL the extra credit.

Finance wasn't really my thing, and I didn't really grasp his lectures, but he is nice and you can definitely talk to him if you need help. LOTS of EC, do all the HW, go to every class, and an A is definitely possible. Got a 56 on one exam but still ended with an A. Do good on the take home exams too.
Great professor, very passionate about finance and a good lecturer.

Professor Virani is a very solid teacher. He's nice and funny and keeps the class engaged well in lectures. Participation counts with in-class questions. His tests consist of an in-class 50 point exam and a take-home 25 point exam for each of the 3 tests. Do every extra credit opportunity given and you'll get an A.

Tough lecture heavy class, but the grading makes it easy to get a good grade. I bombed almost all the in-class exams, but made up the points with the take-home exams. Lots of extra credit is offered and as long as you get 70% on HW you get 100%. As long as you answer questions in class, do well on take home tests, and do the homework, you're good.

Hard to pay attention to lectures, especially because lectures don't translate well to test questions. Absolutely no feedback is given and tests can't be reviewed so it's hard to know what you're doing wrong. Overall difficult to succeed.

Nice guy but lectures are very boring. There is quite a significant difficulty difference between content in class and on the test. What you do in class is not even similar to what is on your exams. You could honestly skip every class and just do his study guides and do okay on the exams. The thing that kept me from this was mandatory attendance.

Best finance teacher I've had. He was very kind, and way more than understanding. It was also very easy to get in touch with him for more help. For exams, the study guides are very helpful, make sure you really study and know them. Attend class so you understand the information. He really tries to help, and make the content relate to real scenarios
Only professor who made my life easier after going online during COVID-19. Also not hard to look at!
Prof. Virani is a great teacher and genuinely cares. I really enjoyed his class, hes also super cute.

Virani inspires interest in the subject matter. Attendance is mandatory because there are clicker q's every class (graded for completeness). Homework load is light, so I would say the class is test heavy. Not a bad class!

Simple, fair class. Last test was way harder than others so be aware. Class was way more fair than the other Eller classes 1st semester. Kinda boring. Also, his attendance policy is a bit dumb bc missing just a single class hurts your grade. Great guy, though. Refers to The Economist and FT articles a lot which is chill.

This class was by far one of the hardest, but one of the most interesting course I have taken. I like how Virani brings in real world topics in his lectures as well as real research going on in finance. Go to class because it's extra credit and do the homework, because he likes to pull a lot of test questions from there.
Great professor !!!

He makes things much more complex than they really are. His tests are not fair at all. He barely gives you time to do them and has made it so you can't go back and check your answers which is probably the worst thing ever. He thinks this will prevent cheating, but everyone still cheats. No point in attending class.
Very caring and smart prof. Loads of EC that isn't easy by any means but is available if you need it. Prof cares more about your learning than giving grades. (White knight of biz)

Dr. Virani was an excellent professor and does a phenomenal job of teaching his content while showing how it is practical in the finance field. Pay attention in class and do your homework and you'll be fine. Most of the test questions come from the homework and the practice tests he provides.

Hilmi Songur.review_text: great class an A is very achievable and he works well with students
He's amazing, great at teaching, and overall a great class. Take Songur for Fin360, you definitely won't regret it.
